description: Core transfer guard branches are untested. grep of src/test for SELF_TRANSFER, isArchived/archived, AUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED/requiresAuthorization, and the BUSINESS-key isFirmAccount ownership check (TransferService lines 89-101) returns no hits in any service test. So the self-transfer 400 (line 170), archived-source/dest 404 (lines 196, 204), requires-authorization 403 (line 212), and BUSINESS firm-account FORBIDDEN 403 (line 98) are all unexercised despite being mainline validation on external, money-moving input. Only the happy path, amount validation, idempotency scoping, and player/name resolution are covered.
recommendation: Add tests: self-transfer rejected; archived source and archived destination each 404; an account with requires_authorization=1 as source or dest yields AUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED (and bypassAuthRequired=true skips it); a BUSINESS token with a fromAccountId not belonging to its firm yields FORBIDDEN.

description: WebhookDispatcherService (293 lines) has no test (grep shows 0 test files reference it). This is the ledger-tailing delivery engine with non-trivial, pin-worthy logic that isn't reachable through the covered WebhookService: the exponential backoff calculation (line 271: min(retryMaxSeconds, retryBaseSeconds * (1L << attempts))), the ingest matching that fans account/firm subscription matches into per-posting enqueues (lines 154-172), the SSRF re-check at send time (line 215), the Discord-vs-signed-event branch (lines 228-242), the toEvent mapping (lines 276-287), and the auto-disable-over-threshold path. The dependencies are all injected mappers/clients, so the pure logic (onFailure attempt/threshold math, toEvent, ingest matching, truncate) is unit-testable without a live HTTP server.
recommendation: Add unit tests with stub mappers for: onFailure attempt-below-max → markRetry with the expected backoff (incl. retryMaxSeconds cap and the 1<<attempts shift), attempt-at-max → markFailed + incrementFailures + disableIfOverThreshold; ingest matching enqueues one delivery per (posting, matching subscription); toEvent field mapping; truncate boundary at 255.

snippet: public AccountByPlayerResponse resolvePlayerAccount(String uuid, String name) {
description: AccountService has no test (grep for getBalance / resolvePlayerAccount / existsGovernmentAccountByName / AMBIGUOUS_NAME in src/test returns nothing; the lone 'AccountService' match in AdminToolsIT is AdminAccountService). Both public methods carry real branching left unverified: getBalance's 404-on-null (lines 42-45); resolvePlayerAccount's exactly-one-of-uuid/name 400 (line 62), malformed-UUID 400 (line 71), PLAYER_NOT_FOUND (line 78), the PAR-144 AMBIGUOUS_NAME government-alt conflict (lines 86-89), and ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND (line 93). The AMBIGUOUS_NAME edge is a permission/ambiguity boundary with documented historical shape and is entirely unpinned.
recommendation: Add an EmbeddedDbIT for AccountService covering getBalance (found + 404) and resolvePlayerAccount for each branch, especially the government-name-collision AMBIGUOUS_NAME 409 (seed a player and a non-archived GOVERNMENT account of the same name).

description: AuthService.rotateToken and adminForceRotate are untested (grep for rotateToken/adminForceRotate/RotateResponse in src/test finds only the ApiKeyMapper.rotateKey stub inside JwtTokenVerifierTest, which tests verification, not rotation). The revoked-key rejection (lines 49-52, 94-96), the concurrent-revoke 0-row race handling (lines 68-75, 106-109), the not-found 404 (line 92), and the fact that a newly minted token verifies are all unverified. These are security-sensitive token-lifecycle paths.
recommendation: Add tests: rotateToken on a live key returns a token that JwtTokenVerifier accepts and whose jti differs from the DB row it replaced; rotate on a revoked/missing key throws TOKEN_REVOKED/KEY_NOT_FOUND; a 0-row rotateKey result surfaces the concurrent-revoke 401.

description: Only 2 of FirmService's 8 public methods are tested. FirmUpdateValidationIT covers updateFirm and updateAccountDisplayName validation; getPublicFirm, getFirmBalance, getFirm, listAccounts, listEmployees, and listRoles are untested. These carry pin-worthy logic: FIRM_NOT_FOUND on unknown display name (lines 48-49, 94-95), the requireBusinessKey scoping guard on the list/get methods, the created_at null-guard (ADT-119, line 59), and listRoles' permsByRole groupingBy/getOrDefault assembly (lines 74-89).
recommendation: Add EmbeddedDbIT coverage: getPublicFirm/getFirmBalance found + FIRM_NOT_FOUND; listRoles assembling permissions per role (incl. a role with no permissions → empty list); a non-BUSINESS token hitting the business-scoped reads is rejected.
verification_note: Snippet matches at FirmService.java:46-49. FirmService has exactly 8 public methods (getPublicFirm:46, listEmployees:63, listRoles:70, getFirmBalance:92, getFirm:101, updateFirm:112, listAccounts:138, updateAccountDisplayName:148; the line-41 constructor is not a business method). Test-source grep shows only firmService.updateFirm (4x) and firmService.updateAccountDisplayName (2x) invoked, both from FirmUpdateValidationIT which constructs FirmService (line 25). The other 6 read-path methods are untested; AdminFirmDocsHiddenIT is a docs-visibility controller test, not FirmService coverage. The pin-worthy logic cited is real and present: FIRM_NOT_FOUND at 48-49 and 94-95, requireBusinessKey scoping on the get/list methods, the ADT-119 created_at null-guard at line 59, and listRoles' groupingBy/mapping/getOrDefault assembly at 74-89. Severity minor is fair: untested read/lookup logic, no money movement, no trigger/ledger invariants at stake. Confirmed.

description: Three SERVICE-scoped admin services have no tests: AdminWebhookService, AdminApiKeyService, and AdminRateLimitService (grep shows 0 test files for each). Their validation and authz guards are unverified: AdminWebhookService.validateSecret's 64-char bound (lines 81-86) and the SSRF gate on create/setUrl; AdminApiKeyService.revoke's KEY_NOT_FOUND (lines 38-39); AdminRateLimitService.setOverride's multiplier>0 and note<=255 checks (lines 34-41). Each also begins with AdminScope.require(verified) whose rejection of non-SERVICE keys is untested here (AdminToolsIT exercises AdminScope only via AdminAccountService/AdminFirmService/TransferService).
recommendation: Add focused tests: AdminRateLimitService rejects multiplier<=0 and over-long note as INVALID_BODY; AdminWebhookService rejects over-64-char secret and non-public URL; AdminApiKeyService.revoke 404s on missing key; each admin service rejects a non-SERVICE token via AdminScope.

description: RateLimitOverrideService is only referenced in tests via a NOOP mapper embedded in RateLimitInterceptorTest (which asserts interceptor behaviour, not this class). Its own resolution logic is unverified directly: null-owner → DEFAULT_MULTIPLIER (line 51), absent override → DEFAULT_MULTIPLIER (line 39), the fail-safe RuntimeException fallback to default (lines 40-45), listAll's empty-on-exception fallback (lines 56-61), and cache invalidation on set/clear (lines 66, 71). The exception-swallowing fallbacks are exactly the kind of quiet correctness path that should be pinned.
recommendation: Add a unit test with a stub ApiRateLimitOverrideMapper: getMultiplier returns default for null owner, for absent override, and when findMultiplier throws; listAll returns empty when findAll throws; set()/clear() invalidate the cached entry (a subsequent getMultiplier re-reads the mapper).

snippet:
private static final String[] DDL = {
            // Minimal stand-ins for the shared economy tables the API reads/writes.
description: The integration-test schema is a hand-maintained DDL stand-in duplicated from economy-flyway rather than applying the authoritative Flyway migrations, and it deliberately omits the trg_postings_ai balance-maintenance trigger (comment lines 137-139). This is real MariaDB (good — no H2 dialect risk), but the stand-in can silently drift from the production schema: a column type/width/nullability or new NOT NULL added in a future V<n> migration won't be reflected here, so mapper tests can pass against a schema production no longer matches. Because the trigger is absent, no test verifies that postings actually update account_balances_mat — the balance-write contract that is the ledger's core invariant is unverified end-to-end.
recommendation: Prefer running the economy-flyway migrations against the embedded MariaDB (Flyway can execute DELIMITER-bearing migrations) so the test schema is the authoritative one, or add a CI check that diffs the stand-in DDL against the latest migration. At minimum, add one test that installs the trigger and asserts a transfer moves the materialised balance.
